channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Latest: Asia and European shares sink as US tariffs take effect
item.description: Asian and European shares slid on Wednesday, with Japan’s Nikkei 225 dipping more than 5%, as the latest set of U.S. tariffs including a massive 104% levy on Chinese imports took effect. The Nikkei 225 lost 3.9% to 31,714.03.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump disrupts global economic order even though the US is dominant
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — By declaring a trade war on the rest of the world, President Donald Trump has panicked global financial markets, raised the risk of a recession and broken the political and economic alliances that made much of the world stable for business after World War II.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many older AAPI adults are foreign-born. They're tough on immigration too, a new poll finds
item.description: A new poll finds sharp generational disagreements among Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders when it comes to how the U.S. should handle mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado Dems are 'poking the bear' by defying Trump's orders, GOP lawmaker warns
item.description: Democrats in Colorado could be on Trump's radar next if a slew of controversial bills dealing with gender and abortion are signed into law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the White House to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Spine of steel', willing to deal
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army adjusts tracker policy and flight training following January collision with passenger jet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army has made changes to how its helicopters use a safety system that broadcasts aircraft location and has reduced the number of flights over Washington following a collision with a passenger jet that killed 67 people, the head of Army aviation told The Associated Press on Tues
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says it cut funding to some life-saving UN food programs by mistake
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department said Tuesday that it had rolled back an undisclosed number of sweeping funding cuts to U.N. World Food Program emergency projects in 14 impoverished countries, saying it had terminated some of the contracts for life-saving aid by mistake.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Army to begin limited use of horse-drawn caissons for Arlington National Cemetery funerals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army will soon begin limited use of horse-drawn caissons for funerals at Arlington National Cemetery, phasing in the long-held tradition after nearly two years of work to improve the care of the horses.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court blocks order requiring Trump administration to reinstate thousands of federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday blocked an order for the Trump administration to return to work thousands of federal employees who were let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans call taxes unfair: Poll
item.description: More Americans say that the amount they will pay in federal income taxes this year is "unfair" than those who say their amount is reasonable, according to a new poll, and most say that corporations and wealthy people are not paying enough.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dow jumps 1,300 points at open, rebounding amid China tariff tension
item.description: Many are waiting to see if the nation’s three major indices take any hits from the escalating trade tension between China and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Growing opposition from House conservatives threatens to derail Trump's agenda
item.description: Growing opposition among hard-line House conservatives to the Senate’s framework for advancing President Trump’s ambitious legislative agenda is threatening to make this week’s vote one of the heaviest lifts yet for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to deport Venezuelans under wartime law, but only after judges' review
item.description: In a bitterly divided 5-4 decision, the court said the administration must give Venezuelans who it claims are gang members “reasonable time” to go to court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Champion steps down as ethics committee chair amid conflict-of-interest questions
item.description: Minnesota Senate President Bobby Joe Champion will step down as head of the Subcommittee on Ethics as he faces questions about a possible conflict of interest related to his legal work and funding bills he has sponsored.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senator says he was joking about using violence against journalists
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., suggested that a lot "less false stories" would be written by journalists if they got into physical altercations with politicians.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
